commands.author: zhangguiping-xydt

target.title: Fix systemd gateway unit scope conflicts
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Closes #79375.
- Fixes Linux systemd gateway handoff when user-scope and system-scope gateway units can both exist after upgrade or scope changes.
- The user-visible failure is dueling gateway services after an upgrade or scope switch: stale user-level units and live system-level units can race each other, kill each other, or leave the gateway in the wrong lifecycle state.

Why does this matter now?

- The linked issue is P1 because this can cause gateway crash loops or message loss on Linux hosts with systemd-managed OpenClaw gateway services.
- The PR was also behind current `main`, so this update rebases the existing fix stack onto current `main` and keeps the diff scoped to the systemd gateway lifecycle code and regression tests.

What is the intended outcome?

- Fix classification: root-cause lifecycle repair that corrects unit selection, handoff, rollback, and cleanup result ownership.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: medium-high for the resolver, retirement, rollback, and cleanup behavior covered by regression tests; medium for final merge because the live proof is intentionally read-only and does not mutate this host's real system-scope units.
- Root cause: the systemd resolver and lifecycle actions previously treated unit identity too narrowly. A stale canonical user unit could hide a live marker-owned system unit, and handoff code did not consistently preserve active/enabled state or distinguish requested system action success from best-effort cleanup failure.
- Source-of-truth boundary: installed gateway ownership is determined from OpenClaw service marker metadata, canonical service names, and live systemd active/enabled state. A stale file existing at the canonical user-unit path is not authoritative by itself.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No CLI/API/config/schema/protocol changes.
- No service-name or unit-path migration.
- No change to systemd privilege boundaries: root is still required for direct system-scope stop/restart, and non-root callers receive sudo guidance.
- No provider, session, channel, or gateway networking behavior changes.
- No attempt to replace maintainer judgment on the related implementation in #91221; this PR remains the existing focused repair for #79375.

What does success look like?

- The resolver selects the live active/enabled gateway unit instead of a stale inactive user unit.
- User-install handoff retires only the relevant conflicting gateway unit and rolls back staged artifacts if retirement or activation fails.
- System-scope restart/stop retires a conflicting user unit before the action when running as root, restores it if the system action fails, and does not report the system action as failed when only post-action cleanup fails.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether marker-owned custom system units are scoped tightly enough for compatibility.
- Whether read-only Linux proof plus the focused regression tests are sufficient, or whether maintainers want an additional isolated VM/container proof that mutates real conflicting user/system units.
- Whether #91221 should supersede this implementation or whether this PR contains useful safer pieces to merge.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #79375.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

- Related PR: #91221, which also targets #79375. Maintainers may need to choose the canonical implementation shape or combine pieces.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- This update responds to ClawSweeper review feedback requesting stronger proof and safer handling around custom systemd units and lifecycle cleanup.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Linux gateway status resolves the installed gateway unit scope without treating a stale canonical user unit as authoritative over a live marker-owned system unit; service lifecycle actions preserve requested system action results while reporting cleanup problems separately.
- Real environment tested: Linux systemd user-service environment with an installed OpenClaw gateway service.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm openclaw gateway status --no-probe --deep --json`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

  ```text
  $ node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway status --no-probe --deep --json
  {
    "cli": {
      "version": "2026.6.2",
      "entrypoint": "openclaw.mjs"
    },
    "service": {
      "label": "systemd user",
      "loaded": true,
      "loadedText": "enabled",
      "notLoadedText": "disabled",
      "runtime": {
        "status": "stopped",
        "state": "inactive",
        "subState": "dead",
        "lastExitStatus": 0,
        "systemd": {
          "unit": "openclaw-gateway.service",
          "killMode": "control-group"
        }
      }
    },
    "config": {
      "cli": {
        "exists": true,
        "valid": true
      },
      "daemon": {
        "exists": true,
        "valid": true
      }
    },
    "gateway": {
      "bindMode": "loopback",
      "bindHost": "127.0.0.1",
      "port": 18789,
      "portSource": "service args",
      "probeNote": "Loopback-only gateway; only local clients can connect."
    },
    "port": {
      "port": 18789,
      "status": "free",
      "listeners": []
    },
    "extraServices": [],
    "pluginVersionDrift": {
      "gatewayVersion": "2026.6.2",
      "drifts": []
    }
  }
  ```

- Observed result after fix: the command completed successfully and reported one installed systemd user gateway service with valid CLI and daemon config, no extra detected gateway services, and no plugin version drift.
- What was not tested: I did not mutate this host's real system-scope gateway service during proof collection.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: destructive handoff, retirement, restore, and cleanup-failure paths are covered by focused regression tests with mocked systemctl calls rather than by changing the real host's system units. The real-environment proof is read-only by design.
- Before evidence: the linked issue describes upgrade/scope changes leaving stale user-level and system-level gateway units that can race or kill each other.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `pnpm vitest run src/daemon/systemd.test.ts`
  - Result: 105 tests passed.
- `pnpm openclaw gateway status --no-probe --deep --json`
  - Result: completed successfully and reported the installed systemd user gateway service and config status.
- Independent pre-submit diff review completed after syncing with current `main` and after the cleanup-failure fix; no further required changes remained.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Active marker-owned custom system unit beats a stale canonical user unit.
- Inactive/disabled marker-owned system unit does not replace the canonical user unit.
- User install retires active marker-owned custom system units and rolls back staged user artifacts when retirement fails.
- Activation rollback disables the replacement user unit and restores the retired system unit.
- System restart retires a conflicting user unit before restarting a system unit when running as root.
- Restart failure restores a retired user unit.
- Non-root restart does not retire the user unit before reporting sudo guidance.
- A successful system restart remains successful when post-action user-unit cleanup fails; cleanup failure is reported separately.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The new cleanup-failure regression failed before the final patch because `fs.unlink` failure after a successful `systemctl restart` propagated as an overall restart failure.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added and updated in the systemd regression suite.

Regression scenario and guardrail rationale:

- The destructive user/system handoff cases are modeled with mocked `systemctl` calls so tests can cover root, non-root, rollback, and filesystem-failure branches deterministically without mutating the developer or CI host's real system units.
- Broad catch/default-null paths are limited to inspection and cleanup boundaries where inaccessible or disappearing systemd state should be reported without overriding the primary lifecycle result. Direct `systemctl` action failures still throw and are covered by regression tests.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes`)

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`No`)

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`No`)

What is the highest-risk area?

- Linux systemd gateway lifecycle control when stale user units and live system units coexist.

How is that risk mitigated?

- Marker-owned system unit detection is limited to Linux system-scope `.service` entries reported by existing OpenClaw service inspection metadata.
- The resolver prefers active/enabled installed units and does not let an inactive/disabled marker-owned unit displace the canonical user unit.
- Retirement and rollback preserve active/enabled state instead of assuming every unit should be re-enabled or restarted.
- Root-only system actions still require root; non-root callers get sudo guidance and do not retire the user unit first.
- Cleanup failures after a successful system restart/stop are reported as cleanup problems, not as failed system actions.
- Regression tests cover success, failure, rollback, cleanup-failure, and non-root boundaries for the lifecycle paths changed here.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Ready for maintainer review after this branch update, subject to maintainer judgment on proof sufficiency and the related #91221 implementation.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Waiting on CI after the branch update.
- Waiting on maintainer judgment on whether read-only Linux proof plus regression coverage is sufficient, or whether a separate isolated VM/container proof that mutates real conflicting units is required.
- Waiting on maintainer judgment on whether #91221 supersedes this PR or should be combined with it.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- RF-001: Mapped and partially addressed. Added/kept Linux read-only systemd proof and detailed the limitation that real destructive conflicting handoff/rollback was not run on this host.
- RF-002: Mapped and partially addressed. PR body now states exactly what was proven live, what remains covered by regression tests, and the proof limitation.
- RF-003: Mapped and addressed in scope. The code path only considers OpenClaw marker-owned system-scope `.service` entries from service inspection metadata and active/enabled state; no unrelated non-marker custom units are retired.
- RF-004: Mapped and partially addressed. Highest-risk handoff/rollback mutations are covered by focused regression tests; the real proof remains read-only.
- RF-005: Mapped and disclosed. #91221 is called out as a related PR requiring maintainer canonical-shape judgment.
- RF-006: Mapped and addressed as author work. The custom-unit compatibility path, rollback behavior, cleanup-failure behavior, proof text, and validation have been updated.
- RF-007: Mapped and addressed. The compatibility boundary is limited to OpenClaw marker-owned system units; unrelated custom units are outside the retirement target.
- RF-008: Mapped and corrected. The PR no longer claims proof is fully sufficient without qualification; it states the live proof and the remaining proof limitation explicitly.
